hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Smart compression with WebP/AVIF conversion. Up to 80% smaller files — free, privacy-first, and watermark-free.

dropzone.maxSize: Max 5MB per file • Up to 10 images per upload

smart.title: Smart Optimization
smart.activeDetail: Auto WebP/AVIF format with optimized quality.

settings.stripMetadata: Strip metadata (EXIF, ICC) for smaller files
settings.stripMetadataDesc: Removing metadata can reduce file size by 10-20% but removes camera information, color profiles, and location data.
settings.formatTip: Single output: Choose one format for optimized results. For multiple formats, use the Convert tool.
settings.qualityExplainer: Most users can leave this at 85% for excellent visual quality with strong compression. Adjust only if you need very small files (lower) or near-lossless output (95-100%).

privacy.notice: All processing local + server-side transient, no files stored.
privacy.title: Data & Privacy
privacy.p1: Your images are processed on-the-fly. Originals stay in your browser; compressed results are streamed back immediately.
privacy.p2: No permanent storage: the server keeps temporary buffers just long enough to encode & deliver the result, then memory is released.
privacy.p3: No analytics on image content; only anonymous aggregate performance counters may be collected (sizes, timings) for optimization.
privacy.p4: Close the tab any time after processing completes. While processing, avoid closing to prevent interruption.

results.settingsChanged: Settings have changed! Click "Apply New Settings" to re-optimize your images with the new configuration.

howTo.title: How to Optimize Images Online
howTo.step1Desc: Drag & drop your files into the dropzone. Works instantly on mobile and desktop without sign-ups.
howTo.step2Desc: Smart mode automatically converts your images to WebP or AVIF for the absolute smallest file size.
howTo.step3Desc: Download images individually or as a ZIP folder. Switch to Manual mode to tweak settings.
howTo.proTipText: Bulk optimize! You can select multiple images at once to compress your entire image batch consistently in seconds.

whyOptimize.title: Why Optimize Your Web Images?
whyOptimize.intro: Fast websites rank higher. Shrinking image file sizes decreases bandwidth, improves your Core Web Vitals, and boosts SEO.
whyOptimize.smartModeEnd: for maximum savings without visible loss.
whyOptimize.manualControlDesc: for precise format, quality, and metadata adjustments.

seo.title: Maximize Website Speed and SEO
seo.lighterPagesDesc: Reduce sizes by up to 80% to cut bandwidth and speed up page load times.
seo.coreWebVitalsDesc: Serve modern formats to improve your LCP and climb search engine rankings.
seo.secureDesc: Images process in your browser and are never uploaded to a remote server.
seo.outro: Use Smart mode for zero-config, effortless results or switch to Manual for strict, granular control over your files.

tips.title: Pro Image Compression Tips
tips.tip1: WebP: Start at 75–85% quality. AVIF: 60–80% is often plenty for great results.
tips.tip2: Need transparency? Prefer PNG or lossless WebP. JPEG flattens backgrounds.
tips.tip3: Keep UI/Text crisp with lossless PNG/WebP. Use lossy WebP/AVIF for standard photos.
tips.tip4: Strip EXIF/ICC metadata under Advanced Settings to shave off another 5-15%.

qualityGuide.title: Quality vs Size Guide
qualityGuide.interactiveDemo: Interactive demo: See how quality affects file size in real-time

items.question: How does the Smart Optimization feature work?
items.answer: Smart Mode automatically selects the best format (WebP/AVIF) and tuning for maximum savings without visible quality loss.

items.question: How much file size reduction can I expect?
items.answer: Typically 50-80% when switching to modern formats like WebP or AVIF, though complex photographs compress better than simple graphics.

items.question: Why should I use this over other image tools?
items.answer: We offer private, all-in-one conversion, compression, and metadata stripping directly in your browser with real-time previews.

items.question: What image formats can I upload and download?
items.answer: Upload JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, and BMP. Download as WebP (broad support), AVIF (best compression), JPEG (older systems), or PNG (transparency).

items.question: Is there a limit to how many images I can compress?
items.answer: You can compress up to 10 images simultaneously, with a 5MB limit per file. You can process unlimited batches.

items.question: Are my images securely processed?
items.answer: Yes. All processing happens entirely within your web browser. Your files never leave your computer and disappear when you close the tab.
